title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
summary: Zack Proser demonstrates a workflow architecture that dispatches AI agents to perform development tasks in isolated environments while the human is away, using signal agents to monitor Slack and Linear, verification gates spanning lint through critic passes, weekly JSONL analysis for inefficiency detection, and physiological monitoring via Oura ring over MCP. The core insight is that human attention, not tool speed, is the constraint.
writeup: Zack Proser of WorkOS walks through a workflow architecture that dispatches AI agents to handle development tasks in isolated environments while the developer is away from the desk. The system pairs signal agents that monitor Slack and Linear with verification gates running from lint through critic passes, weekly JSONL analysis to surface inefficiencies, and physiological monitoring from an Oura ring exposed over Model Context Protocol. The argument is that the binding constraint is human attention rather than tool speed, so the design centers on asynchronous dispatch and automated checks that let work continue unattended. Takeaways include the use of layered verification gates before changes land, log analysis to detect recurring waste, and voice-to-code as an input path.
